items.title: Leak Exposes Members of Peter Thiel’s Secretive ‘Dialog’ Society
items.summary: More than 200 of the world's elites registered for a retreat whose agenda runs from panels on cult-building and sex to prepping for World War III. An associated app offers matchmaking.

items.title: Why the Reflecting Pool Is Full of Algae After Trump's Renovation
items.summary: Warm weather has fueled a bloom that National Park Service workers are trying to kill using everything from hydrogen peroxide to nanobubbles ahead of July 4 celebrations.

items.title: ‘Dangerous’ AI Models Are Coming No Matter What
items.summary: The US government crackdown on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 hides a glaring truth: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.

items.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
items.summary: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

items.title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
items.summary: In the coming weeks, Apple will move anonymously generated emails addresses to a different domain.

items.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
items.summary: SpaceX's valuation has increased by $1 trillion since its shares started trading on Friday.

items.title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
items.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

items.title: Fragile quiet in Lebanon as US-Iran truce leaves unanswered questions
items.summary: Many Lebanese remain doubtful that the agreement could finally mean the end of the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.

items.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
items.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course before the shots were fired.

items.title: Michael Burry says he's tempted to bet against SpaceX, but passes on expensive options
items.summary: Burry argued the company's market capitalization had reached levels that dwarf many established businesses and fortunes.

items.title: Fed Chair Warsh expected to withhold 'dot' from central bank's interest rate outlook
items.summary: The central bank's Federal Open Market Committee is set to release its quarterly update of where individual officials expect interest rates to head.

items.title: Prediction market traders speculate Anthropic will restore access quickly to AI model after Trump admin directed it to limit reach
items.summary: Traders think it's more likely than not that access is restored by July 1.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Senate votes to consider bipartisan housing bill after months of GOP infighting
items.summary: The Senate voted 87-8 on Tuesday to consider a bipartisan housing bill after months of haggling between the House and Senate GOP. The version ultimately agreed to by the House and Senate includes language to limit institutional investors’ ability to buy up single-family homes, which House Republicans had fought to include.  Sen. items.summary: The White House on Tuesday knocked outspoken Trump critic and New York congressional candidate George Conway (D) for airing a congressional campaign ad where he promises to impeach President Trump. “Lightweight George Conway is a stupid person’s idea of a smart person,” White House spokesperson Davis Ingle told The Hill. “His severe and debilitating disease…

items.title: Alien messages may have reached Earth without us realizing it
items.summary: A new SETI study suggests we may be overlooking alien signals not because they aren't there, but because their own stars are scrambling them before they escape into space. Turbulent plasma and powerful stellar storms can spread an ultra-narrow radio transmission across a wider range of frequencies, making it much harder for traditional searches to spot. The effect could be especially important around M-dwarf stars, the most common stars in the Milky Way.

items.title: This strange material can become strong or fall apart in seconds
items.summary: Scientists have found that staple-shaped particles can tangle together to create a material that is both strong and flexible. Unlike conventional materials, these particles can be locked into a sturdy structure or rapidly unraveled using vibrations. The unusual behavior could open the door to recyclable buildings, reconfigurable structures, and even futuristic robotic technologies.

items.title: Scientists just found a hidden weakness in forever chemicals
items.summary: Researchers discovered that hydrogen radicals generated by intense UV light can break down stubborn PFAS “forever chemicals” without added chemicals. The breakthrough reveals a key mechanism that could lead to greener and more effective technologies for permanently destroying these pollutants.

items.title: Chinese money plant leaves hide a mathematical pattern
items.summary: Tiny water-secreting pores appear to organize the major veins of the plant leaves into an arrangement known as a Voronoi diagram.

items.title: A blood test for dementia may tell you if you have more than one type
items.summary: AI helped researchers develop an experimental blood test that might let doctors diagnose overlapping dementias.

items.title: NASA seems to be backing away from hunting for life on Mars
items.summary: Viking 1 kicked off the search for Martian life 50 years ago. Now NASA’s shifting priorities are putting the quest in limbo.
